response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with strong web/full-stack and some systems-level background, but the core requirement of this role is deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation. Their only professional C++ work was a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; since then the career has centered on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no evidence of MQTT/IoT protocol work, message-broker architectures at scale in C++, IEC 62443 or SDL practices, or industrial-cybersecurity exposure, making the fit for a Senior Manager C++ position weak despite solid general seniority.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec with async I/O and fibers)
Broad, senior engineering background with strong testing/craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops)
Event-driven and distributed-systems exposure via the GISCollective platform and Kubernetes deployments
concerns: Minimal and dated professional C++ experience (brief 2014–2015 role); C++ not even listed in current skills
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), or IEC 62443 / SDL security background
Recent trajectory is web/full-stack and AI tooling, diverging from embedded/industrial C++ leadership this role requires

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 exposure
